summary: Automatically staged enrichment from Wikipedia, Wikidata, MusicBrainz.

propertyChanges.value:
Tom Tom Club is an American new wave band founded in 1981 by husband-and-wife team Chris Frantz and Tina Weymouth as a side project from Talking Heads. Their best known songs include the UK top 10 hit "Wordy Rappinghood" and the US top 40 hit "Genius of Love", both from their 1981 debut album, and a cover of The Drifters' "Under the Boardwalk" that reached the UK top 30.

American band

instructions: Act as a music knowledge-graph researcher and editor.
Read all verified evidence across all provided sources before proposing any candidate properties, nodes, or edges.
Only emit ontology-valid candidates with source-backed provenance and concise justification.
Treat every applicable source in sourcePlan as required coverage for this run before broadening beyond the catalog.
Use broad ontology reasoning across people, artists, groups, recordings, venues, labels, and equipment when the evidence supports it.
All applicable catalog sources for this run were already available to the automated pipeline.

dualIdentityRules.note: If a real-world human is an artist, treat Artist and Person as the same identity. Match or create a single dual-labeled node instead of split duplicates.

relationshipDefinitions.description: A recorded track or work is performed by an artist or person.
A work or recording is written by a person.
A recording or release is produced by a person.
A track appears on an album or release.
A recording took place at a studio.
A recording is part of a recording session.
A studio, session, recording, or person used a piece of equipment.
A person or artist played an instrument.
A release or album was released by a label.
A work or release was issued by a label.
A recording or release features another artist or person.
A recording or release was mastered by a person.
A recording or release was engineered by a person.
A person or artist performed on a track, album, or release.
A person or artist is or was a member of a group.
A collection or release contains another work or recording.
Two artists or people collaborated.
An entity was influenced by another entity.
One recording is a cover of another work or recording.
One recording remixes another recording.
A credit entity refers to a person.
A work has a version, variant, or derivative recording.
An entity belongs to a genre.
A performance took place at a venue.
A person or artist participated in a performance or session.
A performer or recording uses an effect.
